data.title: Gold tops stocks as second-favorite long-term investment, real estate still no. 1 - Gallup poll
data.description:
Gold has surpassed stocks as the second-most popular long-term investment choice for Americans, according to a Gallup poll taken last month and released this week, suggesting Americans may be altering their investing habits following a shaky period for the stock market and forecasts of problems ahead for the broader U.S. economy.

Only 16% of Americans now consider stocks the best long-term investment, Gallup said, down six percentage points from a year ago and falling behind buying gold, which rose by five points to become the ideal inverstment for 23% of respondents; real estate remains America's most trusted mode of investment, with 37% believing it is the safest choice for long-term returns.

The poll, which surveyed 1,006 U.S. adults rather than active investors, reflects Americans' perceptions of the safest long-term investment, not active investment choices.

"There's an upturn in U.S. new investors [for gold], and it's becoming notable, but it's still a long way behind the jump that we've already seen in Western Europe," BullionVault director of research Adrian Ash told Newsweek.

Gold prices hit all-time highs above $3,500/oz in late April, up ~40% from a year ago when prices were at $2,200-$2,300/oz.

Gold futures gained more than 1% Friday as the dollar ticked lower, as investors look ahead to the upcoming U.S.-China trade talks this weekend, but despite some positive signs this week, analysts say broader market uncertainty around tariffs still weighs on investor sentiment.

Market watchers say investors are wary of the limited scope of the U.K. agreement and look cautiously at the upcoming meeting between the U.S. and China, as negotiations could be long and drawn-out.

Gold might have been helped Friday by President Trump's Truth Social post that an "80% tariff on China seems right," which would be a considerable reduction from the current 145% but still very high.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for May delivery closed +3.2% this week to $3,335.40/oz, and front-month May silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended +2.1% to $32.676/oz for the week; on Friday, gold gained 1.2% and silver rose 0.9%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: BuildDirect sees Q1 revenue between $14.8M and $15.2M
data.description:
• BuildDirect.com Technologies (TSXV:BILD:CA) on Friday said revenue for Q1 2025 is expected to be in the range of $14.8 million to $15.2 million.
  • Gross profit for Q1 2025 is expected to be in the range of $6.0 million to $6.4 million, while gross profit margin for Q1 2025 is expected to be in the range of 38% to 41%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2025 is expected to be in the range of $0.60 million to $0.65 million.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Globus closes down 23% as earnings misses, lowered EPS guidance weigh
data.description:
• Globus Medical (NYSE:GMED) ended Friday off 23% as a result of missing on both lines in its Q1 earnings results released after Thursday's closing bell and lowering its EPS range for 2025.
  • The medical device company focused on musculoskeletal disorders now sees non-GAAP fully diluted EPS of $3.00-$3.30, which is a decrease from the prior guidance of $3.10-$3.40. Consensus is $3.19.
  • Revenue guidance remains the same at $2.8B-$2.9B. Consensus is $2.83B.
  • In Q1, non-GAAP diluted EPS rose to $0.68 from $0.63 in the year-ago period.
  • Globus ended the quarter (March 31) with ~$461.3M in cash and cash equivalents, compared to $784.4M at the end of 2024.

data.title: Bessent warns debt limit measures could be exhausted in August
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent warned Friday that the federal government could exhaust its cash balance and so-called extraordinary measures by August.

He, in turn, urged Congress to raise or suspend the debt ceiling by mid-July to avoid a potential default while lawmakers will be on break, according to a letter written to House Speaker Mike Johnson.

"After reviewing receipts from the April tax filing season, there is a reasonable probability that the federal government's cash and extraordinary measures will be exhausted in August while Congress is scheduled to be in recess,"

The revised timeline places new pressure on Republicans to finalize a sweeping tax and spending agreement tied to a proposed $5T boost in the borrowing limit.

In early January, the U.S. reached its $36.1B debt limit, prompting the Treasury to deploy extraordinary measures to avoid defaulting on federal obligations.

"A failure to suspend or increase the debt limit would wreak havoc on our financial system and diminish America's security and global leadership position," Bessent said in the letter.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Femto Technologies receives Nasdaq notice to delist
data.description:
• Femto Technologies (NASDAQ:FMTO) has received notification letter from Nasdaq, stating that based on its review of FMTO’s recent private placement transaction that was completed on February 26, 2025, Nasdaq has determined to delist FMTO’s securities.
  • Nasdaq determined that FMTO’s issuance of large numbers of shares upon the cashless exercise and “alternative exercise” of the warrants issued in the placement resulted in significant dilution of existing shareholders.
  • FMTO intends to appeal to a Hearings Panel.
  • Source: Press release

data.description:
• Whitestone REIT (NYSE:WSR) has filed to offer up to $750M in securities, including common shares, preferred shares, depositary shares, and subscription rights.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Vince Holding receives continued listing standard notice from NYSE
data.description:
• Vince Holding (NYSE:VNCE) said on Friday that it has received a notice from the New York Stock Exchange that the company did not presently satisfy NYSE’s continued listing standards, which requires the company’s 30-trading day average market capitalization to be at least $50 million and the company’s stockholders’ equity to be at least $50 million.
  • As set forth in the notice, as of May 5, 2025, the company’s 30-trading day average market capitalization was approximately $22.6 million and the company’s last reported stockholders’ equity, as of February 1, 2025, was approximately $41.8 million.
  • In accordance with applicable NYSE procedures, within 45 days from receipt of the Notice, the Company must submit to NYSE a business plan that demonstrates compliance. The company expects to timely submit such a business plan to the NYSE.
  • Vince Holding (NYSE:VNCE) shares were up more than 17% in extended trading.
